summary: Open match-progress PR that replaced the nonmatching placeholder for `gm_801B0DD0` in `src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c` with a matched implementation and refined `DebugResultsData` in `gm_1B03.static.h`. The function appears to configure the debug results screen by copying cached debug/sound-test values from `un_803FA258`, rebuilding `MatchEnd` standings through `gm_80166A98`, building a character-result fanfare bitmask for non-CPU/non-big-loser standings, then starting result audio and related game-mode setup calls. Evidence reports 1 matched function and 107 failed candidates; no review comments were present.
what_changed: Added field-level layout for `struct DebugResultsData` instead of treating the first 0x2284 bytes as opaque padding.

Recovered bitfield members at the start of `DebugResultsData`: `x0_0 : 1`, `x0_1 : 1`, and `x0_other : 6`, followed by byte fields `x1` through `x4`, three bytes of padding, and a `MatchEnd match_end` member.
Loaded `DebugResultsData` via `gm_801A427C(arg0)` and used its embedded `MatchEnd` as the destination for match-ending/results data.
Copied values from `un_803FA258` indices `0x5A` through `0x5F` into the recovered debug-results fields.
Called `gm_80166A98` with values derived from `un_803FA258` indices `0x51` through `0x60`, including casts to `s8`, `& 0xFF`, and `- 1` adjustments for several player-related values.

Iterated over four `match_end->player_standings` entries and included only players whose `slot_type != 3` and `is_big_loser == 0` when OR-ing character-specific result fanfare bits from `lbAudioAx_80026E84(character_kind)`.
smart_moves: Recovered only the portion of `DebugResultsData` needed by the matched function rather than over-naming the whole former padding block.
Used a leading bitfield layout to match individual bit assignments into the first byte of `DebugResultsData`, preserving the apparent storage shape required for matching.
Kept unknown fields conservatively named (`x0_0`, `x0_1`, `x0_other`, `x1`-`x4`) because the PR evidence does not establish semantic names for them.
Built `sfx_result` as a `u64` OR-mask, matching the audio API usage and avoiding repeated fanfare calls.
Filtered standings before adding result fanfare bits, using the recovered `slot_type`, `is_big_loser`, and `character_kind` fields from `MatchEnd` standings.
decomp_lessons: For game-mode result/debug functions, matching may require reconstructing small leading fields and embedded structs inside formerly opaque per-scene data blocks.

When a function copies data from a global debug buffer such as `un_803FA258`, keep exact indices, casts, masks, and off-by-one adjustments visible; these details are likely matching-sensitive and encode UI/debug-menu storage conventions.
Bitfield recovery can be necessary even when field semantics are unknown; conservative placeholder names are preferable to invented names.
Embedded structs such as `MatchEnd` can be introduced once direct field access proves their offset and usage.
Audio setup for results can be expressed as a character fanfare bitmask assembled from eligible player standings, then submitted through the `lbAudioAx` result-audio functions.
Generated progress PRs may include many failed match candidates; the reusable knowledge here is the one successfully matched function, not the failed list unless investigating future targets.
naming_conventions: Unknown recovered fields in local/static structs used `x`-prefixed offset-style names such as `x0_0`, `x0_1`, `x0_other`, `x1`, `x2`, `x3`, and `x4`.
Recovered aggregate member used the established type name directly: `MatchEnd match_end`.
Function names remained address-based for unmatched/decomp-in-progress game-mode code, e.g. `gm_801B0DD0`, `gm_801A427C`, `gm_80166A98`, `gm_80168FC4`, and `gm_801701A0`.
Local variables were given semantic-but-conservative names where evidence was clear: `sfx_result`, `data`, and `match_end`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `u64 sfx_result = 0` for the accumulated audio bitmask.
Declared loop index separately as `int i`, consistent with older C style often needed for matching.
Used explicit casts to `s8` on several `un_803FA258` entries passed into `gm_80166A98`.
Applied `un_803FA258[0x60] & 0xFF` before passing the first argument after `match_end` to `gm_80166A98`.
Applied `- 1` to `un_803FA258[0x56]`, `[0x57]`, `[0x58]`, and `[0x59]` before passing them to `gm_80166A98`.
Structured the standings loop as `for (i = 0; i < 4; i++)` with a compound `if` checking `slot_type != 3` and `is_big_loser == 0`.
Used bitwise OR assignment with the return value of `lbAudioAx_80026E84` to accumulate character result sound bits.
Included `PAD_STACK(8)` at the end, indicating stack padding was required for the match.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/progress text reports `gm_1B03.c` `gm_801B0DD0` as matched at 100% and 107 other functions as failed candidates.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching `gm_1B03.c` or `DebugResultsData` to see whether `x0_0`, `x0_1`, `x1`-`x4`, and `x0_other` receive semantic names.
Search for additional matches of functions that consume `un_803FA258` indices near `0x51`-`0x60` to clarify the debug-results parameter layout.
Search for `gm_80166A98` callers to understand the exact meaning and order of the match standings parameters.
Search for `lbAudioAx_80026E84`, `lbAudioAx_80026F2C`, and `lbAudioAx_8002702C` usage to document result/victory fanfare audio conventions.
Investigate later closure or merge status for PR #2489 because the provided slice shows it as open.
